title: Implement API key authentication and wallet-level rate limiting
markdown:
## Goal

Add API key authentication for server-to-server integrations and rate limiting at the wallet server layer. Today, the wallet server only supports session-based JWT authentication initiated by human users via password or Google OAuth. Server-to-server use cases (e.g., a backend service writing data on behalf of a user, a CI/CD pipeline, a cron job) have no headless authentication path. Additionally, there is no rate limiting, so a single client can overwhelm the server.

## Background

The wallet server's credential system (`libs/b3nd-wallet-server/credentials.ts`) uses an extensible handler registry:
- `PasswordCredentialHandler` -- password-based signup/login
- `GoogleCredentialHandler` -- Google OAuth signup/login
- `registerCredentialHandler(type, handler)` -- allows registering new types

This extensibility means API key authentication can be implemented as a new `ApiKeyCredentialHandler` registered with type `"api-key"`. However, API keys differ from user credentials:
- They are long-lived (no session, no expiration)
- They authenticate a service, not a user (but act on behalf of a user)
- They should be revocable without changing the user's password
- They should have optional scope restrictions (read-only, specific URI prefixes)

For rate limiting, the Hono framework (used in `WalletServerCore`) supports middleware. A rate limiter middleware can be added to the CORS/logging middleware chain in `core.ts`. Rate limits should be per-IP or per-API-key with configurable windows and thresholds.

Current middleware chain in `core.ts`:
1. CORS middleware (`cors()`)
2. Request logging middleware (logs method, path, status, duration)
3. Route handlers

Rate limiting would be inserted after CORS but before route handlers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] API key generation endpoint: `POST /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey` (authenticated, creates a new API key for the current user)
- [ ] API key authentication: requests with `Authorization: ApiKey <key>` header are authenticated
- [ ] API key metadata stored at obfuscated path with signed+encrypted payload (name, scopes, createdAt, lastUsedAt)
- [ ] API key listing endpoint: `GET /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey` (lists user's API keys, without showing the secret)
- [ ] API key revocation endpoint: `DELETE /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey/:keyId` (revokes a specific key)
- [ ] API keys support optional scope restrictions: `readOnly`, `uriPrefixes` (limit which URIs the key can access)
- [ ] Rate limiting middleware added to `WalletServerCore` with configurable limits
- [ ] Rate limits configurable per endpoint category (auth: stricter, proxy: standard, health: lenient)
- [ ] Rate limit responses return `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header
- [ ] `WalletServerConfig` updated with `rateLimits` configuration object
- [ ] `WalletClientInterface` updated with API key management methods
- [ ] Tests for API key CRUD, authentication, scope enforcement, and rate limiting

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/credentials.ts` to understand the `CredentialHandler` interface, `CredentialContext`, and the handler registry pattern.

2. Read `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts` to understand the middleware chain and how JWT verification works in route handlers. API key verification will be an alternative auth path.

3. Read `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts` to understand obfuscated path conventions for storing credentials. API key records will follow the same pattern.

4. Add API key types to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export interface ApiKeyConfig {
     name: string;
     scopes?: ApiKeyScope;
   }
   export interface ApiKeyScope {
     readOnly?: boolean;
     uriPrefixes?: string[];
   }
   export interface ApiKeyRecord {
     keyId: string;
     keyHash: string; // SHA-256 hash of the API key (never store plaintext)
     name: string;
     scopes: ApiKeyScope;
     createdAt: string;
     lastUsedAt: string | null;
   }
   ```

5. Add rate limit types to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export interface RateLimitConfig {
     enabled: boolean;
     windowMs: number;     // Time window in milliseconds (default: 60000)
     maxRequests: number;  // Max requests per window (default: 100)
     authMaxRequests?: number;  // Stricter limit for auth endpoints (default: 10)
   }
   ```

6. Implement API key management functions in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts`:
   - `createApiKey(client, serverPublicKey, username, config, ...)` -- generate random key, store hashed version at obfuscated path, return plaintext key (shown once)
   - `verifyApiKey(client, serverPublicKey, apiKey, ...)` -- hash the key, look up record, verify
   - `listApiKeys(client, serverPublicKey, username, ...)` -- list keys without secrets
   - `revokeApiKey(client, serverPublicKey, username, keyId, ...)` -- delete key record

7. Add API key routes in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`:
   - `POST /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey` -- JWT-authenticated, creates API key
   - `GET /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey` -- JWT-authenticated, lists keys
   - `DELETE /api/v1/auth/api-keys/:appKey/:keyId` -- JWT-authenticated, revokes key

8. Add auth middleware extraction in `core.ts` to support both JWT and API key:
   - If `Authorization: Bearer <token>` -- verify JWT (existing behavior)
   - If `Authorization: ApiKey <key>` -- verify API key, resolve username
   - Proxy routes check scopes (readOnly, uriPrefixes) after authentication

9. Implement rate limiting middleware in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`:
   - In-memory sliding window counter keyed by IP (or API key if authenticated)
   - Insert middleware after CORS, before routes
   - Different limits for auth vs proxy vs health endpoints
   - Return 429 with `Retry-After` header when exceeded

10. Update `WalletServerConfig` with optional `rateLimits: RateLimitConfig` field.

11. Update `WalletClientInterface` in `libs/b3nd-wallet/types.ts` and implement in `libs/b3nd-wallet/client.ts`.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts` -- Add `ApiKeyConfig`, `ApiKeyScope`, `ApiKeyRecord`, `RateLimitConfig`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts` -- Add `createApiKey()`, `verifyApiKey()`, `listApiKeys()`, `revokeApiKey()`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts` -- Add API key routes, dual auth middleware, rate limiting middleware
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/mod.ts` -- Export new types and functions
- `libs/b3nd-wallet/types.ts` -- Add API key management types to `WalletClientInterface`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet/client.ts` -- Implement API key management methods

## Testing

```bash
deno test libs/b3nd-wallet-server/ --ignore=libs/b3nd-client-localstorage
deno test libs/b3nd-wallet/
```

Specific test scenarios:
- Create API key returns a plaintext key that authenticates subsequent requests
- API key with `readOnly: true` rejects proxy write requests
- API key with `uriPrefixes: ["mutable://invoices/"]` rejects reads outside that prefix
- Revoked API key returns 401
- Rate limiter returns 429 after exceeding threshold
- Rate limiter returns `Retry-After` header
- Rate limiter resets after window expires
- Auth endpoints have stricter limits than proxy endpoints

## Dependencies

Depends on G5-A9-01 (email/profile) for the updated profile storage structure. API keys are stored alongside user profile data using the same obfuscated path conventions.
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Email-based password reset
- [ ] User profile management (display name, avatar)
- [ ] API key authentication (server-to-server)
- [ ] Rate limiting
- [ ] Audit logging
- [ ] Key rotation support
- [ ] Multi-factor authentication
- [ ] WebAuthn/passkey support
